DECLARATION OF STEPHEN M. KAPPOS FILED BY PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT WESTERN COLLECTION BUREAU

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS KEVIN B. LANIGAN & SIAVASH NAZHAND'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT, IF ENTERED, AND QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PURSUANT TO CCP 473.5, THE MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT AND THE MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS ON DEFENDANT LANIGAN AND DEFENDANT NAZHAND ARE GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S PROOFS OF SERVICE FORMS CREATES A REBUTTABLE PRESUMPTION THAT SERVICE WAS PROPER, BUT DEFENDANTS REBUT THE PRESUMPTION BY THEIR DECLARATIONS THAT THEY DID NOT RECEIVE ACTUAL NOTICE OF THE COMPLAINT, WERE NOT SERVED, AND THAT THE PERSONS PURPORTEDLY SERVED DO NOT MATCH DESCRIPTIONS OF ANY OCCUPANTS OR THEMSELVES. ALTHOUGH THE DECLARATIONS ARE NOT ARTFULLY DRAFTED, THERE IS A STATEMENT UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY UNDER THE LAWS AND THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA ON THE (UNNUMBERED) PAGE ENTITLED "ACTUAL KNOWLEDGE OF THE LAWSUIT," WHICH THE COURT CONSTRUES AS APPLYING TO THE TWO PREVIOUS PAGES ENTITLED "DECLARATION." DEFENDANTS PREPARED A PROPOSED ANSWER (WITH THE HEADING "EXHIBIT A - ATTACHMENT TO PARAGRAPH 11"), WHICH DENIES THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT, IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 473.5(B), ALTHOUGH THE ANSWER, WHEN FILED, MUST HAVE A CAPTION PAGE WITH THE CASE NAME AND NUMBER AND TITLE OF PLEADING, AND OTHERWISE CONFORM TO THE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. FURTHER, THE COURT ORDERS DEFENDANTS TO FILE AN ANSWER BY JANUARY 10, 2014. ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185 (302/MJM)

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-30-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS KEVIN B. LANIGAN & SIAVASH NAZHAND'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT, IF ENTERED, AND QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS

Appearances: Douglas B. Provencher of Provencher & Flatt LLP (707.284.2380) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party; Kevin B. Lanigan and Siavash Nazhand (650.926.9739) defendants and moving parties appearing in propria persona.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Pursuant to CCP 473.5, the motion to set aside/vacate judgment and the motion to quash service of summons on defendant Lanigan and defendant Nazhand are granted. Plaintiff's proofs of service forms creates a rebuttable presumption that service was proper, but Defendants rebut the presumption by their declarations that they did not receive actual notice of the complaint, were not served, and that the persons purportedly served do not match descriptions of any occupants or themselves. Although the declarations are not artfully drafted, there is a statement under penalty of perjury under the laws and the state of California on the (unnumbered) page entitled "Actual Knowledge of the Lawsuit," which the court construes as applying to the two previous pages entitled "Declaration." Defendants prepared a proposed answer (with the heading "Exhibit A - attachment to Paragraph 11"), which denies the allegations of the complaint, in compliance with CCP 473.5(b), although the answer, when filed, must have a caption page with the case name and number and title of pleading, and otherwise conform to the California Rules of Court. Further, the Court orders defendants to file an Answer by January 10, 2014. Order signed after hearing. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185 (302/MJM)

Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances on TUE-OCT-07-2013:
KEVIN B. LANIGAN, defendant appearing in Propria Persona;
SIAVASH NAZHAND, defendant appearing in Propria Persona

The defendants appearing in pro per did not notify the Court by 4PM on October 7, 2013 of their opposition to the Court's tentative ruling. The Court adopts and modifies its tentative ruling re Plaintiff CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT WESTERN COLLECTION BUREAU'S Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions.

The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: NO OPPOSITION HAVING BEEN FILED, PLAINTIFF, CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO POST-JUDGMENT DISCOVERY IS GRANTED AND MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $360.00 ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANTS. The Court shall issue an order. Judge Pro Tem: Paul A. Renne; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
